summary:
Set 035 Session 2 of 4 — writer alignment + glossary harvest. Two deliverables. (1) Writer parity check: 10-row table verifies tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/cancelLifecycle.ts (TS) and ai_router/session_lifecycle.py (Python) produce byte-equivalent on-disk shape across all canonical concerns (filename constants; `# Cancellation history` header; ISO-8601 local-seconds timestamp with ±HH:MM offset; LF-only newlines; UTF-8 no BOM; PID-uniquified temp + atomic rename; self-terminating prepend `<verb> on <iso>
<reason>

`; state-file flip status='cancelled' + preCancelStatus=<prior> with re-cancel preserving original; restore inference fallback Set 7 backfill rules; JSON.stringify indent=2 vs json.dumps indent=2 both + trailing newline). No drift. Table committed to glossary-harvest.md disposition section. (2) Glossary harvest: new docs/session-sets/035-.../scripts/harvest_glossary.py — one-shot tool that walks the solution looking for filename-like literals (regex ` [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_.\-]{0,63}\.(md|json|jsonl|toml|yaml|yml|txt|html) `), groups by extension, clusters by Levenshtein distance <= 3 via union-find, ranks clusters that touch a curated CANONICAL_MARKERS set. Output: glossary-harvest.md (706 lines, 40 clusters across 5 extension buckets). Triage outcome: ZERO in-session fixes. All five canonical-touching clusters triaged as (a) acceptable variance: Session-State.json/Session-state.json (Title Case heading + sentence-start prose in lessons-learned.md + project-guidance.md); Package.json (heading + bullet-start prose in 3 historical proposal/spec/spec-delta docs); SPEC.md/Spec.md (docs/screenshots/SPEC.md is distinct Set-011 artifact + Spec.md prose comments); CHANGELOG.md vs change-log.md (DIFFERENT canonical artifacts — PyPI/Marketplace release log vs per-session-set aggregation); CANCELLED.md/_cancelled.md/cancelled.md (trigger case is moot post-Session-1 — state-file-first reader ignores stray markers; all non-canonical occurrences are self-references or historical artifacts in closed Set 033). The 'Resolution: _cancelled.md mismatch' section documents the future-contributor guidance (stray markdown is harmless audit history; safe to delete or ignore). C1 follow-on logged: Python CLI print_session_set_status at ai_router/__init__.py:935 still calls is_cancelled(path) file-presence-first, mirroring the TS pattern Session 1 just migrated; recommended scope (add read_cancellation_state to session_lifecycle.py, refactor one CLI caller, add four parity tests, bump dabbler-ai-router patch version) flagged for operator decision at Set 035 close. 6 new writer-parity tests added (LF-only bytes / no BOM; JSON serialization shape; cancel preserves 11 sibling keys via deep equality; round-trip parametric over not-started/in-progress/complete; timestamp regex + negative asserts on ms fractions + UTC Z; re-cancel after restore preserves original preCancelStatus). All 6 green. Full extension suite: 462 passing, 2 pre-existing unrelated failures unchanged. ai_router/tests/test_session_lifecycle.py: 17 passed. Typecheck clean. Verification: Round A via gemini-pro VERIFIED, no must-fix issues ($0.0232; bundle 48,542 chars). Round B not needed. Cumulative routed Set 035 spend: $0.0394 of $0.50 forecast. Next session (Session 3 of 4): documentation alignment + Layer-3 Playwright coverage.

reason.specifics: Session 3 (documentation alignment + Layer-3 Playwright coverage) builds directly on Session 2's writer-parity contract and the state-file-first reader contract from Session 1; same engine + identity keeps the H2 architectural model and the new readCancellationState contract continuous across the documentation + rendering verification work. Layer-3 Playwright scenarios assert what the operator sees painted on screen, which benefits from the same orchestrator that locked the test patterns in Session 1.
